close_reason: Hand-rolled DER parser handles only SubjectPublicKeyInfo extraction; der crate dependency would be heavier than needed
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:102-183 implements a minimal ECDSA DER signature parser from scratch. The rustcrypto/formats 'der' crate (and the 'ecdsa' crate's Signature::from_der / to_bytes pair) handle this in one method call with strict-DER conformance, fully tested.

What goes wrong now: F5 and F6 are real correctness gaps in the hand-rolled parser. The 'ecdsa' crate's Signature::from_der already enforces minimal-length encoding and rejects trailing bytes — adopting it eliminates those bugs and removes ~80 lines of code from this crate.

The dev-deps already contain 'p256', which re-exports ecdsa::Signature::from_der and Signature::to_bytes. Promote p256 to a runtime dep gated on the ecdsa-p256 algorithm, and replace ecdsa_der_to_raw_p256 with:
  use p256::ecdsa::Signature;
  let sig = Signature::from_der(&sig_bytes).map_err(...)?;
  Ok(sig.to_bytes().to_vec())

What goes wrong if we don't fix: this crate carries a hand-rolled DER parser that other security-sensitive crates already solve more robustly; future maintainers may copy/paste the laxity into other contexts (see F5).
title: Consider depending on rustcrypto/formats 'der' crate instead of hand-rolled DER parser
